JV Launch, Part 2
How do you build relationships with joint venture partners or potential joint venture
partners?
I mentioned this in the last video - it’s all about adding value.
I’m just going to walk through some of the ways you can do that. How do you add
value? One of them is you could buy their product, it’s a great one. Buy their product.
Buy their high end product, whatever their top of the line product is, go ahead and buy
the product, use the product, tell them how you used it. People love to hear about
their customers, their clients getting results. Go tell them how you used to it, one of
the absolute best ways to get on someone’s radar. Another way is comment on their
blog. If they’re publishing a blog go and leave comments, leave intelligent comments.
You never want to be a stalker.
That doesn’t work so well, but leave intelligent comments on their blog. Read their
blog so you get to know them, get to know who they are, what they care about
because as you get to know them that allows you to interact with them and make
better comments. Comment on their social media. If they’re publishing whatever on
Facebook, on Twitter, on Vine, it doesn’t matter. Wherever they’re publishing, go in
there and leave comments. You don’t have to leave a comment every single post
because that can be a little weird, but leave comments and potentially leave intelligent
comments. Add to the conversation, help create a conversation then you can write
about them on your blog.
Again you don’t want to every blog post to be about them, but every now and then
especially if they do something cool, write about them, create a blog about them
because the deal is they’re probably got a Google alert set on their name. If someone
writes about them then they are going to hear about it. It’s a reticular activating
system. The reticular activating system that’s the thing in your brain that if you’re in a
party, it could be a crowded party and 20 conversations going on in the room, but if
someone says your name across the room then somehow you can magically hear that
even among all these … Boom your name, boom, you hear it. It’s the same way when
someone sees someone wrote about them on the web or in social media.
There’s no way they cannot go there and read what you wrote. Another thing you
could do to is use track back, if you blog about them it could track back to them. On
social media you could write about them and they will absolutely hear about you if you
tag them. This can be great ways to just get on their radar so they know who you are.
Another method (this is probably the best way to get their attention) is to sell their
stuff.
Join their affiliate program and become a good affiliate and sell their product even if
you have to … If you’ve picked one person that you really want to build that
relationship with and this is all about picking who you want, just go all out even if you
have to create some massive bonus package to be able to sell their product. You need
to think strategically, think long term. Here is Fred and I want to get on his radar and I
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module6v1.0

-- 2 of 4 --

want it the way I’m going to do this if I sell a bunch of his product then go create a
massive, massive bonus package that you’re going to give to anyone who buys Fred’s
product through your affiliate link.
Buy your way, when I say buy I mean really put that effort in so you could sell a bunch
of their product and buy your way onto their radar by selling a bunch of their product.
There’s now other ways to add value as well. What do they want? What interests do
they have? Who do they want to meet? When I say interests, something like guitars. I
like to play guitar. I’m not a great guitarist, but I really appreciate really great guitarists
and I had a potential partner gave me an extraordinary guitar. For those of you who
know guitars it’s a Gibson ES-335. Go Google that. If you’re not a guitar player go
Google it, Gibson ES-335 and see what they cost.
They’re not cheap, I’m talking a full authentic Gibson here, not an inexpensive guitar. I
love to ski, I’m a skier. I love to mountain bike. I’ve had someone basically say they’re
willing to take me heli-skiing, skiing on a helicopter where you take a helicopter up the
top of a mountain, not an inexpensive proposition. There are ways people are adding
value to my life. I want it like back in the old days when I first rolled out product
launch formula and it was clear I had some real stature in the market even though I
didn’t have a product out yet, I actually put together as part of prelaunch a teleseminar
and I had something like 6,000-8,000 people register for that call and it turned out my
teleseminar line wasn’t big enough.
I got a call out of the blue by this guy by the name of Mike Filsaime, back then no one
had heard of him. Mike’s a guru now, but no one had heard of him then. Mike called
me up and said “Hey I have a bridge line and how about if we patch my bridge line into
your bridge line and that will give you an extra 500 spots.” I’d heard of Mike, but I
didn’t know him at all really. He just called completely out of the blue and gets ahold of
me and offers me this when I’m in a real time of need “Hey I’ve got this bridge line.”
Boom, needless to say I built a relationship with Mike after that because that was such
a big hearted thing to do and that’s the way Mike is.
He’s got this incredibly huge heart and he’s so helpful for people and low and behold
three or four months later when he did a product launch, guess who was promoting it?
I was promoting it. There’s all kinds. Figure out how can you add value to your life?
There’s all different kinds of ways. Another one, Erico, one of my platinum members
bought 700 copies of Brendon Burchard’s book and he’s sending it to all of his clients.
That’s a really good way to get on Brendon’s radar. Erico told me all about that I sent
Brendon an email and said “Brendon check out what Erico just did for you, bought 700
copies of your book at retail.”
All different kinds of ways to get on people’s radar, but buy their product, do good with
it, report back to them, sell their product, sell a bunch of their product, two really good
ways.
One of the things what I call the JV food chain, a lot of people come up to me and say
“Jeff how do I get you to promote or how do I get the number one guru in my market
to promote.” It’s usually the number one guru or the top, the A players, they’re going
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 3
PLF_Module6v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 4 --

to be tough to get on their radar. It’s going to be tough to get on their calendar
because they’ve got so many people coming to them that want to promote. The way
you get on the A player’s radar is it’s all about especially the JV food chain goes like
this.
Here’s you’re A players, your B players, your C players, your D players, your E players.
You can define these however you want and I’m not judging people’s relative works, I’m
just talking about their stature in the market and their power in the market. Going to
the A players can be tough. Start at the top in any business it’s hard to start at the
very, very top.
It’s a lot easier to get on their radar of the E players. It’s just a lot easier, they have
less people coming to them, you get on their radar. The thing about those E level
players is, who do they hang out with? They hang out with other E level players. They
also know some of the D players.
If you do a deal with the E player and you do really good for them and make them a
bunch of money and you have a great product and you send them the money on time
and everything like that, you could probably get an introduction to some of the D
players. The D players are probably watching what the E players are doing. Then you
work your way up to the D players and you create some real value for them. Who do
the D players hang out with? They hang out with other D players, but they also know
some C players. If you do really good for the D players then you can use it to get on
that radar of the C players. You can see that you can keep on doing this and leapfrog
your way up to the A players.
Is this a short term thing where you’re going to have the biggest person in the market
promoting, biggest A player promoting for you next week? Absolutely not, this is a long
term play, but it absolutely works and I’ve seen it work over and over. Everything about
this game and especially everything about the joint venture team is in long term
process. It doesn’t happen overnight, but it does happen.
It’s all about building you’re positioning over time and building momentum
over time and it will absolutely work for you.
